source.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids - Health Professional Fact Sheet

content.definition: Omega-3s are polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) with a carbon-carbon double bond located three carbons from the methyl end.

types.name: ALA (Alpha-linolenic acid)
types.note: Essential fatty acid; body converts to EPA/DHA at less than 15% efficiency

types.name: DHA (Docosahexaenoic acid)
types.note: Concentrated in brain and retina tissue

cardiovascular.summary: Higher consumption associated with lower heart failure risk

key_trials.name: REDUCE-IT
key_trials.finding: High-dose EPA (4g/day) reduced cardiovascular events by 25%

key_trials.name: STRENGTH
key_trials.finding: No benefit from combined EPA/DHA formulation

cardiovascular.AHA_recommendation: 1-2 seafood servings weekly; 1g/day EPA+DHA for existing coronary disease

pregnancy_infant.note: DHA accumulates rapidly in fetal brain during third trimester

cancer.VITAL_trial: No significant effect on cancer incidence or mortality for breast, prostate, or colorectal cancers

cognitive_function.clinical_trials: No benefits shown in cognitively healthy older adults; may modestly improve mild cognitive impairment

AMD.AREDS2_trial: LC omega-3 supplementation for up to 5 years did not reduce AMD progression risk

health_benefits.dry_eye: Mixed results; no consensus on optimal dose
health_benefits.rheumatoid_arthritis: Reduces anti-inflammatory drug and corticosteroid use; inconsistent effects on pain and swelling

supplement_types.note: Phospholipid form; bioavailability comparable to fish oil

safety.high_dose_concern: High doses (2-15g/day) may increase bleeding time
safety.atrial_fibrillation_risk: 4g/day slightly increased atrial fibrillation risk in CVD patients

drug_interactions.warfarin_anticoagulants: Antiplatelet effects; 3-6g/day doesn't significantly affect INR but periodic monitoring warranted
